description: Labeled corpus for the pairs/refs v2 FPR gate (Phase 5 of bt-vxu9 / bt-gkyn). Synthesized from real shared-Dolt state: 5 intentional cross-project pairs (bt-zsy8/bd-zsy8; Gastown 4-way bt-byk+cass-byk+tpane-byk+cnvs-byk; dotfiles-2il/mkt-2il; dotfiles-x08/mkt-x08; 3-way bd-fjip/cass-fjip/mkt-fjip) plus 6 suffix-collision negatives picked from the 11 v1-detected collisions (153, 1hk, 52t, 9c9, cv6, dyg). Each intentional pair carries cross-prefix dep edges; collisions carry none. Prose samples exercise each --sigils mode (strict/verb/permissive) with both intentional references and negative cases (placeholder IDs, English slugs, fenced-code exclusions). Prefix naming reflects the public-repo exposure policy documented in docs/design/2026-04-21-pairs-refs-v2.md: only bt/bd/cass/mkt/dotfiles/tpane/cnvs/cctui/portfolio/sms/updoots appear; no further aliasing required. Sanitized via scripts/audit-corpus.sh before commit.

issues.title: Ref source: strict-mode sigils
issues.description: Validating strict-mode detection. See [bd-zsy8](https://example.invalid/bd-zsy8) and inline-code `cass-byk`; also ref: mkt-2il. The string bt-xxx should be ignored (placeholder text, no matching target).
issues.notes: Additional ref in notes: refs: dotfiles-x08.

issues.title: Ref source: verb-mode sigils
issues.description: Validating verb-proximity detection. This issue blocks bd-fjip until the column lands; paired with mkt-fjip for the hook surface. see cass-fjip for the client. An unrelated prose mention of mkt-x08-only should not fire a verb match because -only is an English slug.

issues.title: Ref source: permissive-mode and exclusions
issues.description:
Permissive mode recognizes bare mentions like bd-zsy8 or cass-byk in prose. Inside a fenced block the IDs should NOT fire:

```
bt-xxx and cass-xyz
```

After the fence the next bare id is mkt-fjip which should fire in permissive mode.
issues.notes: Intentional inline-code exclusion: the identifier `bt-suppressed` should not fire because it is inline code AND its suffix is not a real bead; but the sigil kind would be inline_code if it were.

issues.title: Ref source: broken external dep
issues.description: Exercises scanDepsV2 with an unresolved external: dep.

issues.title: Ref source: closed target (stale)
issues.description: Mentions [bt-zsy8](https://example.invalid/bt-zsy8) which is closed. Should fire with stale flag.

issues.title: Ref source: negatives only
issues.description: Contains only non-ref text. Mentions `sessions-only` and `-side` and the placeholder bt-xxx, none of which should emit under strict or verb mode.
